headline: Mark Zuckerberg Uses An Unusual Hiring Rule At Meta — And It Starts With One Question He Asks Himself: 'How Do You Know That Someone Is Good Enough?'
summary: Mark Zuckerberg says he only hires someone if he believes, in a different scenario, he would be willing to work for them — a personal test he uses to ensure exceptional talent and leadership potential at Meta.

headline: Tesla Owner Says Car Drove His Wife To Hospital All The Way After She Got Into Labor Unpredictably: 'Definitely A Great Car'
summary: Tesla's FSD system helped an owner's wife get to the hospital while she was in labor, AI lead Ashok Elluswamy weighs in.

headline: SoFi Tech, Opendoor, Rivian— Investors Couldn't Stop Talking About These And More Stocks This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week (Nov. 10-14) on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets: OPEN, RIVN, NVDA, SOFI, BBAI.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Nvidia, CoreWeave, Oklo — And AI-Linked Stocks Keep Falling
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Apple CEO Tim Cook Could Reportedly Step Down As Soon As Next Year— Inside iPhone Maker's Push To Position John Ternus As Successor
summary: Apple is reportedly ramping up preparations for a leadership transition, with the board and senior executives intensifying discussions around identifying Tim Cook's successor.

headline: Elon Musk Reveals That He Pressures TSMC And Samsung To Speed Up Tesla's AI Chip Output: 'Five Years To Me Is An Eternity'
summary: Elon Musk says Tesla is pressuring chipmakers TSMC and Samsung to drastically speed up production of its next-generation AI chips, warning that traditional five-year semiconductor timelines are far too slow for the company's aggressive ambitions.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg-Led Meta To Reportedly Grade Employees On 'AI-Driven Impact' As Big Tech Declares Human-AI Hybrid Work 'No Longer Optional' (UPDATED)
summary: Meta Platforms Inc is reportedly preparing to evaluate employees based on their ability to drive results using artificial intelligence.

headline: After Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, Kevin O'Leary Says China Threatens US AI Leadership Because Of This One Thing— And It's Not Chips
summary: Kevin O'Leary warns that China's rapid power infrastructure expansion—not chip production—is giving it a decisive advantage over the U.S. in the global AI race.

headline: September Jobs Report Set For Thursday Release As Government Shutdown Data Fog Lifts
summary: The first economic report after the government shutdown, September's nonfarm payrolls, highlights missing data and uncertainty for markets and the Federal Reserve.
